entities.name: PEANUT
entities.description: Major oilseed and staple food crop facing production challenges.

entities.name: U.S. PEANUT GENOME INITIATIVE
entities.description: Initiative launched in 2004 to coordinate international efforts in genome research.

entities.name: GENOME RESEARCH
entities.description: Scientific study of the genetic makeup of peanut.

entities.name: MOLECULAR MARKER DEVELOPMENT
entities.description: The process of creating molecular markers to improve genetic map resolution and coverage.

entities.name: GENETIC MAP RESOLUTION
entities.description: The precision and detail of the genetic map of a crop.

entities.name: INTERNATIONAL PEANUT GENOME INITIATIVE
entities.description: Global research community effort to meet peanut genome research needs.

entities.name: PEANUT GENOME SEQUENCING PROJECT
entities.description: Project initiated in 2010 to sequence the peanut genome.

entities.name: PEANUT GENOME CONSORTIUM
entities.description: Organization that initiated the peanut genome sequencing project.

entities.name: DIPLOID PEANUT PROGENITORS
entities.description: Ancestral diploid species from which peanut is derived.

entities.name: MARKER-ASSISTED BREEDING
entities.description: Breeding technique using molecular markers to develop improved genotypes.

entities.name: GENETIC MAPPING
entities.description: Process of identifying the location of genes on a chromosome.

entities.name: TRAIT MAPPING
entities.description: Process of mapping specific traits to genetic locations.

entities.name: MULTI-PARENTAL GENETIC MAPPING POPULATIONS
entities.description: Advanced populations used for high-resolution genetic and trait mapping.

entities.name: BI-PARENTAL POPULATIONS
entities.description: Populations derived from two parents used for genetic mapping.
